events.title: MakerDAO launches Single-Collateral Dai (Sai)
events.description: MakerDAO activated the Dai Credit System via MKR governance vote, letting users lock ETH as collateral to mint the DAI stablecoin in a fully on-chain, market-driven way (no token sale).
events.impact: Created the first widely used decentralized stablecoin and the CDP/over-collateralized lending primitive, becoming a foundational money-lego that virtually all later DeFi protocols built upon.

events.title: Uniswap v1 launches on Ethereum mainnet
events.description: Hayden Adams deployed Uniswap v1, an automated market maker (AMM) using the constant-product (x*y=k) formula, enabling permissionless token swaps and liquidity provision without order books.
events.impact: Established the AMM as DeFi's dominant exchange model and made it trivial for anyone to list a token or supply liquidity, seeding the on-chain trading infrastructure that powered later booms.

events.title: MakerDAO migrates to Multi-Collateral Dai (MCD) with the Dai Savings Rate
events.description: Maker upgraded from single-collateral Sai to Multi-Collateral Dai, accepting multiple collateral types and introducing the Dai Savings Rate (DSR) for passive yield on DAI.
events.impact: Made DAI more scalable and resilient, and introduced an on-chain native yield primitive that strengthened DAI's role as DeFi's base trading and collateral asset.

events.title: Compound launches COMP liquidity mining, igniting DeFi Summer
events.description: Compound began distributing ~2,880 COMP governance tokens daily to lenders and borrowers (split 50/50), rewarding users for supplying and borrowing assets. COMP spiked to ~$372 within days and briefly became the largest DeFi token.
events.impact: Invented the liquidity-mining / yield-farming playbook that became DeFi's standard go-to-market, triggering 'DeFi Summer 2020' and a wave of copycat token launches (Balancer, Curve, etc.).

events.title: Yearn Finance 'fair launch' of YFI
events.description: Andre Cronje launched the YFI governance token with zero allocation to the team or VCs; tokens could only be earned by providing liquidity. YFI reached a ~$100M market cap within a week.
events.impact: Popularized the 'fair launch' ethos and yield aggregation/vaults, intensifying DeFi Summer's farming mania and influencing community-first token distribution models.

events.title: SushiSwap's 'vampire attack' on Uniswap
events.description: SushiSwap forked Uniswap's code, rewarded users who staked Uniswap LP tokens with SUSHI, then migrated over $1B of liquidity away from Uniswap.
events.impact: Demonstrated the 'vampire attack' liquidity-siphoning tactic and added fee-sharing/governance incentives, pressuring incumbents and accelerating the competitive token-incentive arms race.

events.title: Uniswap launches UNI governance token with retroactive airdrop
events.description: Uniswap distributed 400 UNI (~$1,344 at the time) to every wallet that had used the protocol before Sept 1, 2020, reaching over 250,000 addresses; 15% of supply went to past users.
events.impact: Set the gold standard for retroactive airdrops (later copied by Optimism, dYdX, ENS, and many others) and cemented governance tokens as a core user-acquisition and decentralization tool.

events.title: OlympusDAO (OHM) launches Protocol-Owned Liquidity and (3,3)
events.description: Anonymous founder 'Zeus' launched OlympusDAO, pioneering bonding and Protocol-Owned Liquidity (POL) instead of rented liquidity-mining, with the viral (3,3) staking game-theory meme and very high staking APYs.
events.impact: Sparked the 'DeFi 2.0' wave of POL/bonding designs and reflexive high-yield tokenomics; influenced many forks but also previewed the unsustainability of hyper-inflationary yields.

events.title: Uniswap v3 launches with concentrated liquidity
events.description: Uniswap v3 went live on Ethereum mainnet, letting liquidity providers concentrate capital within custom price ranges for far greater capital efficiency than the full-range v2 model.
events.impact: Dramatically improved AMM capital efficiency and reshaped LP strategy (active management, range orders), becoming the template for the next generation of DEXs.

events.title: Curve Wars peak and DeFi TVL all-time high
events.description: Protocols (Convex, Yearn, Redacted Cartel) battled to accumulate veCRV voting power over Curve's stablecoin liquidity emissions, birthing the 'bribe' economy. Total DeFi TVL peaked around this period, reaching an all-time high near $180B in November and ~$250B+ by early December 2021 per DeFiLlama.
events.impact: Showcased meta-governance and vote-bribery as a new DeFi primitive and marked the cyclical top of the 2021 bull market before the 2022 unwind.

events.title: Terra/UST algorithmic stablecoin collapse
events.description: TerraUSD (UST), an algorithmic stablecoin propped up by ~20% Anchor yields and its sister token LUNA, lost its $1 peg starting May 7-9 and entered a death spiral; LUNA fell from ~$80-116 to near zero and the chain was halted, wiping out roughly $40-50B in days.
events.impact: Triggered the worst DeFi/crypto contagion of the cycle, discredited algorithmic stablecoins, and set off cascading insolvencies (3AC, Celsius, Voyager) and an extended 'crypto winter.'

events.title: Celsius freezes withdrawals
events.description: Crypto lender Celsius Network paused all customer withdrawals, swaps, and transfers amid a liquidity crisis worsened by the Terra fallout and risky DeFi/staking exposures.
events.impact: Signaled deep contagion from Terra into the centralized-lending sector and froze billions in customer funds, deepening the bear market and counterparty distrust.

events.title: Three Arrows Capital (3AC) ordered into liquidation
events.description: After heavy losses on LUNA, staked-ETH, and leveraged bets, the BVI High Court ordered the liquidation of crypto hedge fund 3AC; it filed for Chapter 15 in the U.S. on July 1, facing ~$3.5B in creditor claims.
events.impact: 3AC's default cascaded through its many lender counterparties (Voyager, BlockFi, Genesis, Celsius), turning the Terra shock into a systemic credit crisis across crypto.

events.title: Celsius files for Chapter 11 bankruptcy
events.description: Celsius Network and affiliates filed for Chapter 11 in the Southern District of New York, about a month after freezing withdrawals.
events.impact: Cemented the collapse of the CeFi lending model and reinforced DeFi advocates' 'not your keys, not your coins' / transparency arguments, even as overall crypto sentiment cratered.

events.title: Ethereum completes The Merge (Proof-of-Stake)
events.description: Ethereum transitioned from proof-of-work to proof-of-stake, cutting energy use by ~99.95% and making staked ETH a native yield-bearing asset (though withdrawals were not yet enabled).
events.impact: Laid the groundwork for the liquid-staking and later restaking economy that would dominate DeFi yield in 2023-2024.

events.title: FTX collapses and files for bankruptcy
events.description: After a Nov 2 CoinDesk report exposed Alameda Research's balance sheet was loaded with FTX's own FTT token, Binance dumped FTT and a run ensued; FTX halted withdrawals (Nov 8) and filed Chapter 11 on Nov 11, with SBF resigning and an ~$8B customer shortfall revealed.
events.impact: The defining event of the bear market: it destroyed trust in centralized exchanges/CeFi, took down Genesis/BlockFi and others, and accelerated the regulatory crackdown and the narrative shift toward self-custody and on-chain (DeFi) transparency.

events.title: USDC de-pegs after Silicon Valley Bank failure
events.description: Circle revealed that $3.3B of USDC reserves (~8%) were stuck at the failed Silicon Valley Bank; USDC briefly fell to ~$0.87, dragging DAI and other USDC-collateralized stablecoins off peg before the U.S. backstopped SVB depositors and USDC recovered.
events.impact: Exposed DeFi's deep dependence on USDC as collateral and the fragility of even 'fully reserved' stablecoins to traditional banking risk, spurring reserve diversification and tokenized-Treasury demand.

events.title: Ethereum Shapella upgrade enables staking withdrawals; LSDfi era begins
events.description: The Shanghai/Capella ('Shapella') upgrade finalized at ~22:42 UTC, finally allowing validators to withdraw staked ETH and rewards, removing the key risk of locked ETH.
events.impact: De-risked liquid staking and ignited 'LSDfi' (Lido stETH, Rocket Pool rETH used across lending/AMMs), which became the dominant DeFi TVL category and the foundation for restaking.

events.title: EigenLayer launches restaking on Ethereum mainnet
events.description: EigenLayer went live (initial mainnet/early-access in June 2023), letting users 'restake' ETH or liquid-staking tokens to extend Ethereum's economic security to other services (AVSs).
events.impact: Created the 'restaking' primitive and a new shared-security marketplace, becoming one of DeFi's fastest-growing categories and the centerpiece of the 2024 points/airdrop meta.

events.title: Coinbase launches Base mainnet to the public
events.description: Coinbase opened its OP-Stack Layer-2 network Base to the general public (builder access since July 13), celebrated with the month-long 'Onchain Summer' campaign.
events.impact: Brought a major regulated U.S. exchange's distribution to an L2, dramatically lowering fees and seeding a new wave of consumer DeFi, memecoins, and 'onchain' apps over 2024-2025.

events.title: SEC approves 11 U.S. spot Bitcoin ETFs
events.description: The SEC approved spot Bitcoin ETF applications from BlackRock, Fidelity, 21Shares, and others, which began trading the next day amid a fee-competition price war.
events.impact: Opened a regulated TradFi on-ramp that pulled institutional capital into crypto, lifting the whole market and indirectly boosting DeFi liquidity and the institutional-DeFi narrative.

events.title: BlackRock launches BUIDL tokenized money-market fund
events.description: BlackRock, via Securitize, launched the BUIDL tokenized Treasury/money-market fund on Ethereum; it grew from ~$40M at launch to over $1.8B (later ~$2.9B) and expanded to multiple chains.
events.impact: Validated real-world-asset (RWA) tokenization at institutional scale, establishing tokenized U.S. Treasuries as a flagship RWA category and a major new source of on-chain yield/collateral.

events.title: EigenLayer mainnet goes fully operational with first AVS (EigenDA)
events.description: EigenLayer became fully operational, launching EigenDA as its first Actively Validated Service; restaking TVL climbed toward ~$15B and dozens of AVSs followed.
events.impact: Turned restaking into a live shared-security market and fueled a points/airdrop farming frenzy that defined 2024 DeFi capital flows.

events.title: EigenLayer EIGEN token stakedrop begins
events.description: EigenLayer began distributing its EIGEN governance token via a multi-season stakedrop (Season 1 from a March 15 snapshot), with tokens becoming transferable in late September 2024.
events.impact: One of the largest airdrops of 2024, it crystallized the 'points farming' meta and the economics of the restaking sector while drawing criticism over allocations and geo-restrictions.

events.title: U.S. spot Ethereum ETFs begin trading
events.description: After the SEC declared the registration statements effective on July 22, spot Ether ETFs from BlackRock, Fidelity, Grayscale, Bitwise, and others started trading July 23.
events.impact: Extended regulated institutional access to ETH, the settlement and collateral asset of most DeFi, reinforcing ETH's institutional legitimacy and the staking/restaking economy around it.

events.title: Hyperliquid HYPE airdrop launches the perp-DEX era
events.description: Hyperliquid, a high-performance perpetuals DEX on its own L1, airdropped ~310M HYPE (~31% of supply, worth >$1.6B) to ~94,000 users in a VC-free, community-first distribution.
events.impact: One of the most valuable airdrops ever; it propelled Hyperliquid to dominate on-chain perpetuals volume and showcased a high-performance, app-specific-chain model for institutional-grade on-chain trading.

events.title: Bybit hacked for ~$1.5B by North Korea's Lazarus Group
events.description: Attackers compromised the Safe multisig wallet interface used by exchange Bybit, tricking signers into approving malicious transactions and draining ~$1.5B in ETH-the largest crypto heist in history, attributed by the FBI to Lazarus.
events.impact: Underscored persistent infrastructure/supply-chain and signing-UI risks even for sophisticated custodians, intensifying focus on security tooling and on-chain monitoring across DeFi and CeFi.

events.title: GENIUS Act signed into law: first U.S. federal stablecoin framework
events.description: President Trump signed the GENIUS Act (S. 1582) after Senate (June 17, 68-30) and House (July 17, 308-122) passage, establishing federal licensing for payment-stablecoin issuers and requiring high-quality liquid reserves (cash/short-term Treasuries) and monthly reserve disclosures.
events.impact: The first U.S. federal crypto law to take effect, it gave regulatory clarity to dollar stablecoins-DeFi's core liquidity layer-encouraging bank and institutional issuance and accelerating the institutional-DeFi/RWA era.

events.title: RWA tokenization reaches ~$20B; institutional DeFi consolidates
events.description: By the end of 2025, total on-chain tokenized real-world assets (excluding stablecoins) reached roughly $20B (up from ~$5.5B at the start of the year), led by tokenized Treasuries/money-market funds (BlackRock BUIDL, Ondo, OpenEden) as Wall Street's main gateway to DeFi; the figure climbed to ~$25B by March 2026 and ~$32B by mid-2026 per rwa.xyz.
events.impact: Marked the maturation of RWAs as a durable, regulation-friendly DeFi vertical, blending TradFi yield with on-chain composability and anchoring the 2025-26 institutional-DeFi era.

eras.name: Genesis / Early DeFi
eras.summary: MakerDAO's DAI and Uniswap v1 establish the first decentralized stablecoin, lending, and AMM money-legos that define the building blocks of DeFi.

eras.name: DeFi Summer
eras.summary: Compound's COMP liquidity mining ignites yield farming; Yearn's fair launch, SushiSwap's vampire attack, and the Uniswap UNI airdrop turn governance tokens and incentives into DeFi's growth engine.

eras.name: 2021 Bull Market & DeFi 2.0
eras.summary: Uniswap v3 concentrated liquidity, OlympusDAO's protocol-owned liquidity, and the Curve Wars drive TVL to record highs amid reflexive yields and meta-governance.

eras.name: The Great Unwind / Crypto Winter
eras.summary: Terra/UST's implosion cascades through 3AC, Celsius, and Voyager, and culminates in FTX's collapse-the deepest contagion of the cycle and a crisis of trust in CeFi.

eras.name: Recovery & LSDfi
eras.summary: After the USDC/SVB scare, Ethereum's Shapella upgrade unlocks staking withdrawals, liquid-staking (LSDfi) becomes DeFi's dominant yield, and Base launches as a major consumer L2.

eras.name: Restaking, L2s & ETFs
eras.summary: EigenLayer restaking and a points/airdrop meta surge, spot Bitcoin and Ethereum ETFs bring institutional capital, and Hyperliquid's HYPE airdrop launches the high-performance perp-DEX era.

eras.name: RWA, Stablecoin Law & Institutional DeFi
eras.summary: The GENIUS Act gives U.S. stablecoins legal clarity, tokenized Treasuries/RWAs (excluding stablecoins) scale to ~$20B by end-2025 and ~$32B by mid-2026, and institutional DeFi (Hyperliquid, BUIDL, on-chain credit) matures-while the record Bybit hack keeps security front of mind.
